title: geranium humile subsp. kauaiense
intro: Geranium humile subsp. kauaiense, also known as Kauai cranesbill, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant endemic to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Geranium humile subsp. kauaiense include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, although it is essential to note that this plant is rare and endangered, and its use should be done under the guidance of a qualified practitioner.
